slides.title: Python's Giant App Store
slides.body: Python comes with a solid standard library, but the real magic is **PyPI** — over 500,000 free packages built by developers worldwide. Need to fetch a web page or crunch data? Someone already wrote it.
slides.tip: PyPI stands for Python Package Index and lives at pypi.org.

slides.title: Install Anything in One Command
slides.body: Run `pip install <package-name>` in your **terminal** and pip downloads and installs the package automatically. It's the install button for Python code.
slides.code:
# In your terminal (not a Python file):
pip install requests

slides.title: Import and Use It Instantly
slides.body: After installing, use `import` just like any built-in module. Here `requests` fetches a live URL with a single function call.
slides.tip: Status code 200 means the request succeeded.

slides.title: Lock Your Dependencies
slides.body: Share your project and teammates need the same packages. Run `pip freeze > requirements.txt` to snapshot every package and version, then anyone can restore them with one command.
slides.code:
# Save dependencies:
pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Restore on another machine:
pip install -r requirements.txt

slides.title: Keep Projects Isolated
slides.body: You've got the full toolkit: **install** with `pip`, **import** in your code, and **share** via `requirements.txt`. Use a **virtual environment** (`python -m venv .venv`) so each project keeps its own packages and nothing ever clashes.
slides.tip: Virtual environments are the professional standard — set one up for every real project.
